content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Dr. Manuel M. Lopez 
City of Oxnard 
300 W. Third Street 
Oxnard, CA 
93030 
Dear Dr. Lopez: 
September 5, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our Advice File No. I-86-263 
We have received a copy of your request for advice to 
Michael Bradbury, District Attorney for the County of ventura. 
In a footnote to that letter you are asking us to confirm 
earlier advice given to you over the telephone regarding a 
potential conflict between concurrent membership on the grand 
jury and participating on a campaign committee for re-election 
in a municipal election. 
The Political Reform Act does not address incompatibility of 
public offices. 
The Attorney General has published numerous 
opinions on this subject. 
Those opinions are listed in the 
attached Bulletin and may be obtained by calling (916) 324-5172. 
If we can be of further assistance to you, please feel free 
to call the Legal Division at (916) 322-5901. 
JET:km 
Sincerel , 
J~ette ~. Turvill 
Legal Assistant 
Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804..0807 • (916)322-5660 


CITY OF 
naro 
iM 
CITY COUNCIL. 300 W. THIRD ST .• OXNARD, CALIFORNIA 93030 • (805) 486-4311 EXT. 3550 
OR. MA.NUEl M. LOPEZ, COUNCILMA.N 
August 15, 1986 
Michael Bradbury, District Attorney 
800 S. Victoria Ave. 
Ventura, CA. 93003 
Dear Mr. Bradbury, 
I am a canidate for re-election to the city council in Oxnard in 
November 1986. 
Recently, in a discussion, an individual who has been extremely helpful 
to me in past campaigns raised a concern regarding potential, or per-
ceived, conflict of interest in concurrent membership on the grand jury 
and a campaign committee. 
I called the Fair Political Practices Commission in Sacramento and they 
adviced me via phone that there was no conflict in their opinion, however, 
that they were primarily concerned with economic conflicts of interest, 
therefore, I should more properly further inquire of your office for other 
potential conflicts. 
My question is: 
Does membership in the grand jury preclude active con-
current participation on a committee for re-election in a municipal election? 
Sincerely, 
Im~~ 
IS 
Dr. Manuel M. Lopez 
\ 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Dr. Manuel M. Lopez 
city of Oxnard 
City Council 
300 W. Third street 
Oxnard, CA 
93030 
Dear Dr. Lopez: 
August 19, 1986 
Re: 
86-263 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received on August 19, 1986 by the Fair Political 
Practices commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
JET:plh 
(
r; ) truly yours, 
r1~(~ 
tJva1~:i-LU(V 
J~nette E. Turvil{/ 
Legal Assistant 
Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • 
Sacramento CA 9S804~0807 • 
(916) 322~5660

sections.question: Does membership in the grand jury preclude active concurrent participation on a committee for re-election in a municipal election?
sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act does not address incompatibility of public offices. The Attorney General has published numerous opinions on this subject. Those opinions are listed in the attached Bulletin and may be obtained by calling (916) 324-5172.
sections.question_synthetic: Dr. Lopez asks whether serving on a grand jury creates a conflict of interest that would prevent him from simultaneously serving on his own campaign committee for municipal re-election.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the Political Reform Act does not specifically address conflicts arising from concurrent public office memberships, and directed the requester to Attorney General opinions on incompatibility of offices.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated August 15, 1986) and the FPPC's acknowledgment/response (dated August 19 and September 5, 1986). The substantive response declining to address office incompatibility issues was issued September 5, 1986.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does membership in the grand jury preclude active concurrent participation on a committee for re-election in a municipal election?

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not address incompatibility of public offices. The Attorney General has published numerous opinions on this subject. Those opinions are listed in the attached Bulletin and may be obtained by calling (916) 324-5172.

embedding.summary: Dr. Manuel M. Lopez requested FPPC advice regarding potential conflicts between concurrent grand jury service and participation on his campaign committee for municipal re-election. The FPPC responded that the Political Reform Act does not address office incompatibility and referred him to Attorney General opinions on the subject.
